 {"id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb.14","title":"[Install] dcg doctor diagnostic command","description":"# Task: dcg doctor Diagnostic Command

## Background
Diagnostic command helps users troubleshoot installation issues.

## Checks Performed
1. Binary health (version, location, permissions)
2. Configuration validity
3. Agent detection status
4. Hook status per agent
5. Database health
6. Performance benchmark

## Output Format
Clear status with checkmarks, warnings, errors.

## Options
- --fix: Auto-fix resolvable issues
- --json: Machine-readable output

## Success Criteria
- All checks complete in < 2 seconds
- Clear, actionable output
- --fix resolves common issues","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T15:37:27.471280031-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T15:37:27.471280031-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb.14","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T15:37:27.472908638-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

## Background
Verify system requirements before installing to prevent partial installations.

## Checks
1. OS/arch compatibility
2. Disk space (minimum 10MB)
3. Write permissions
4. Existing installation detection
5. Agent detection (informational)
6. Network connectivity (if downloading)

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb.2","title":"[Install] AI agent detection functions","description":"Add functions to detect installed AI coding agents.

## Detection Matrix

| Agent | Directory | Command | Config File |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| Claude Code | ~/.claude | claude | ~/.claude/settings.json |
| Codex CLI | ~/.codex | codex | ~/.codex/config.toml |
| Gemini CLI | ~/.gemini-cli | gemini | ~/.gemini-cli/settings.yaml |
| Aider | - | aider | ~/.aider.conf.yml |
| Continue | ~/.continue | continue | ~/.continue/config.json |

## Implementation

```bash
# Returns array of detected agents
detect_agents() {
  DETECTED_AGENTS=()
  
  # Claude Code
  if [[ -d "$HOME/.claude" ]] || command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
    DETECTED_AGENTS+=("claude-code")
    if command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
      CLAUDE_VERSION=$(claude --version 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo "unknown")
    fi
  fi
  
  # Codex CLI  
  if [[ -d "$HOME/.codex" ]] || command -v codex &>/dev/null; then
    DETECTED_AGENTS+=("codex-cli")
  fi
  
  # Gemini CLI
  if [[ -d "$HOME/.gemini-cli" ]] || command -v gemini &>/dev/null; then
    DETECTED_AGENTS+=("gemini-cli")
  fi
  
  # Aider
  if command -v aider &>/dev/null; then
    DETECTED_AGENTS+=("aider")
  fi
  
  # Continue
  if [[ -d "$HOME/.continue" ]]; then
    DETECTED_AGENTS+=("continue")
  fi
}

print_detected_agents() {
  if [[ ${#DETECTED_AGENTS[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
    info "No AI coding agents detected"
    return
  fi
  
  info "Detected AI Coding Agents:"
  for agent in "${DETECTED_AGENTS[@]}"; do
    case "$agent" in
      claude-code) echo "  ✓ Claude Code${CLAUDE_VERSION:+ (v$CLAUDE_VERSION)}" ;;
      codex-cli) echo "  ✓ Codex CLI" ;;
      gemini-cli) echo "  ✓ Gemini CLI" ;;
      aider) echo "  ✓ Aider" ;;
      continue) echo "  ✓ Continue" ;;
    esac
  done
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- All five agents detectable
- Version shown when available
- Clean output with gum styling
- Works when agents partially installed (dir but no command)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:54:46.677356458-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:54:46.677356458-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:54:46.678991178-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb.3","title":"[Install] Claude Code auto-configuration","description":"Automatically configure DCG as a PreToolUse hook for Claude Code.

## Claude Code Hook Configuration

Location: ~/.claude/settings.json

Hook format for PreToolUse hook that blocks dangerous Bash commands.

## Implementation

The configure_claude_code function should:
1. Check if settings.json exists, create if not
2. Check if DCG hook already exists (idempotent check)
3. Backup existing config before modification
4. Add the PreToolUse hook using jq
5. Handle both cases: hooks array exists or does not exist

## Rollback Support

Keep backup: ~/.claude/settings.json.backup.<timestamp>

Provide uninstall command: dcg uninstall --agent claude-code

## Acceptance Criteria

- Hook added to settings.json correctly
- Existing hooks preserved
- Already-configured is no-op (idempotent)
- Backup created before modification
- Works with missing hooks array
- jq dependency handled gracefully","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:55:04.684974664-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:55:04.684974664-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:55:04.686489588-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:55:31.065914232-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb.5","title":"[Install] Full idempotency for install.sh","description":"Make install.sh fully idempotent - safe to run multiple times.

## Idempotency Requirements

1. Binary Installation
   - Check if dcg exists and matches target version
   - Skip download if current
   - Replace only if version differs

2. Agent Configuration  
   - Check if hook already configured before adding
   - Never duplicate hooks
   - Preserve existing settings

3. PATH Configuration
   - Check if already in PATH before adding
   - Dont duplicate PATH entries in shell rc files

## Implementation Pattern

Each operation should follow:
1. Check current state
2. If already in target state, log and return
3. If not, make change
4. Verify change succeeded

## Verification Script

Create dcg doctor command that verifies installation state:
- Binary exists and is executable
- Version matches expected
- Hooks configured for detected agents
- PATH includes binary location

## Acceptance Criteria

- Running install.sh twice produces identical state
- No duplicate hooks in agent configs
- No duplicate PATH entries
- Clear logging of skipped operations","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:55:05.240714683-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:55:05.240714683-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:55:05.24187059-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:55:31.209424699-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:55:31.272177699-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-dxs","title":"[CATEGORY] platform.github - GitHub Platform Pack","description":"# GitHub Platform Pack

## Overview
GitHub is the primary code hosting platform for most developers. Actions, webhooks, deploy keys, and repository settings can cause significant damage if misconfigured or deleted.

## Why This Pack
- GitHub Actions secrets contain sensitive credentials
- Deploy keys provide repository access
- Webhook deletion breaks CI/CD pipelines
- Repository deletion is catastrophic
- Branch protection removal exposes main branch

## Key Destructive Patterns

### gh CLI
- `gh repo delete` - Repository deletion
- `gh secret remove` - Removes Actions secrets
- `gh variable remove` - Removes Actions variables
- `gh repo deploy-key delete` - Removes deploy keys
- `gh api -X DELETE` - Raw API deletions
- `gh release delete` - Deletes releases
- `gh run cancel` - Cancels workflow runs

### API Patterns
- `DELETE /repos/{owner}/{repo}`
- `DELETE /repos/.../actions/secrets/`
- `DELETE /repos/.../hooks/`
- `DELETE /repos/.../keys/`

## Safe Patterns
- `gh repo view`, `gh repo list`
- `gh secret list`, `gh variable list`
- `gh api` with GET method
- `gh status`, `gh auth status`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 2 Epic (git_safety_guard-hhh)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] gh CLI patterns comprehensive
- [ ] API endpoint patterns detected
- [ ] Tests cover common destructive operations
- [ ] Safe operations not blocked","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:28:23.042704709-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T23:05:24.108230595-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T23:05:24.108230595-05:00","close_reason":"platform.github pack implemented and registered, all dependencies closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-dxs","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-3w5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:34:29.797817078-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-dxs","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.494880713-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-dxs","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.54457833-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-dxs","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.590843759-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-dxs","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.636752267-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-dxs","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.680932382-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-dxs","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.726444594-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-e2eh","title":"Extend e2e_test.sh with heredoc test cases","description":"# E2E Test Script Extension for Heredocs

## Objective

Extend the existing `scripts/e2e_test.sh` with comprehensive heredoc test cases.

## Test Categories to Add

### 1. Heredoc Detection Tests

```bash
# Should BLOCK - Python heredoc with os.system
test_blocked 'python3 << EOF
import os
os.system("rm -rf /tmp/test")
EOF' "Python heredoc with os.system"

# Should BLOCK - Bash heredoc with rm
test_blocked 'bash << EOF
rm -rf /important
EOF' "Bash heredoc with rm -rf"

# Should BLOCK - Node inline script
test_blocked 'node -e "require(\x27child_process\x27).execSync(\x27rm -rf /\x27)"' "Node inline with execSync"
```

### 2. Heredoc Variant Tests

```bash
# Test all heredoc syntaxes
test_blocked 'cat <<EOF | python3
import os; os.system("rm -rf /")
EOF' "Piped heredoc"

test_blocked 'python3 <<-EOF
    import os
    os.system("rm -rf /")
EOF' "Indented heredoc (<<-)"

test_blocked 'python3 <<\x27EOF\x27
import os
os.system("rm -rf /")
EOF' "Quoted delimiter heredoc"
```

### 3. Language Coverage Tests

```bash
# Python
test_blocked 'python3 -c "import shutil; shutil.rmtree(\x27/tmp\x27)"'

# Ruby  
test_blocked 'ruby -e "FileUtils.rm_rf(\x27/tmp\x27)"'

# Perl
test_blocked 'perl -e "system(\x27rm -rf /\x27)"'

# JavaScript
test_blocked 'node -e "require(\x27fs\x27).rmdirSync(\x27/\x27, {recursive:true})"'
```

### 4. False Positive Tests

```bash
# Should ALLOW - Documentation with dangerous strings
test_allowed 'bd create --description="This pattern blocks rm -rf"' "Doc with rm -rf"
test_allowed 'git commit -m "Fix git reset --hard detection"' "Commit msg"
test_allowed 'grep "rm -rf" src/main.rs' "Grep for pattern"
test_allowed 'echo "example: git push --force"' "Echo example"
```

### 5. Bypass Attempt Tests

```bash
# Variable indirection
test_blocked 'bash -c "CMD=rm; $CMD -rf /"' "Variable indirection"

# Base64 encoding
test_blocked 'bash -c "echo cm0gLXJmIC8K | base64 -d | bash"' "Base64 bypass"

# String concatenation  
test_blocked 'python3 -c "exec(\x27r\x27+\x27m\x27+\x27 -rf /\x27)"' "String concat"
```

### 6. Edge Cases

```bash
# Empty heredoc
test_allowed 'cat << EOF
EOF' "Empty heredoc"

# Safe heredoc content
test_allowed 'python3 << EOF
print("hello world")
EOF' "Safe Python heredoc"

# Nested quotes
test_blocked 'bash -c "bash -c \x27rm -rf /\x27"' "Nested shells"
```

## Logging Enhancement

Add verbose mode:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

VERBOSE=${VERBOSE:-0}
LOG_FILE="e2e_test_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"

log() {
    echo "[$(date '+%H:%M:%S')] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

test_blocked() {
    local cmd="$1"
    local desc="$2"
    log "TEST: $desc"
    log "  CMD: ${cmd:0:80}..."

    if echo "{\"tool_name\":\"Bash\",\"tool_input\":{\"command\":\"$cmd\"}}" | ./target/release/dcg 2>/dev/null; then
        log "  FAIL: Expected block, got allow"
        return 1
    else
        log "  PASS: Correctly blocked"
        return 0
    fi
}
```

## Output Summary

At end of test run:
```
=====================================
E2E Test Summary
=====================================
Total tests: 150
Passed: 148
Failed: 2
Skipped: 0

Failed tests:
  - Python heredoc with subprocess (line 234)
  - Node inline with dynamic require (line 456)

Log file: e2e_test_20260107_193045.log
=====================================
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- `scripts/e2e_test.sh` contains a dedicated heredoc section with:
  - true-positive blocks across at least 3 languages
  - false-positive regression cases (doc/test strings)
  - a small set of bypass-attempt cases
- Verbose logging is available and includes per-test:
  - test name
  - normalized command (truncated)
  - decision (allow/deny) and rule attribution when denied
  - failure diagnostics sufficient for CI triage
- The heredoc E2E suite runs deterministically and completes in a reasonable time budget (define target after baseline).
","notes":"Adding multi-language heredoc/inline E2E coverage + logging improvements now that e7m is integrated.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonFalcon","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:00:00-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T06:52:18.249005975-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T06:52:18.249005975-05:00","close_reason":"Expanded scripts/e2e_test.sh heredoc/inline suite to cover multi-language blocks (node/python/ruby/perl/bash), added safe heredoc cases + safe-string trigger regression, and improved verbose logs to surface rule attribution from denial reason.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e2eh","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:28:32.841249285-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e2eh","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:08:55.268336107-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e2eh","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:50:08.884862029-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-e4fl","title":"AI Agent Ergonomics & MCP Integration","description":"# Epic: AI Agent Ergonomics & MCP Integration

## Priority Score: 750/1000 (High Priority)

## Background and Rationale

DCG is a security tool designed for AI coding agents. The research found that modern AI agents (Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI) expect:
1. Structured JSON output with predictable schemas
2. Clear, actionable error messages
3. Consistent exit codes
4. MCP-compatible tool interfaces
5. Machine-readable documentation

This epic ensures DCG is a first-class citizen in the AI coding agent ecosystem.

## Research Findings (2025-2026)

### Claude Code Hook Protocol
- Exit 0: Normal operation (check JSON for decision)
- Exit 2: Blocking error (stderr shown to Claude)
- JSON output only processed on exit 0
- permissionDecision: allow | deny | ask

### Emerging Standards
- AGENTS.md: Machine-readable context for AI agents (20k+ repos)
- Agent Client Protocol (ACP): JSON-RPC over stdio
- MCP November 2025 spec: Structured tool outputs with JSON Schema

### Key Patterns
- --format json flag on all commands
- Structured error objects with codes
- Remediation suggestions in JSON
- Idempotent operations

## Technical Approach

1. Enhance HookSpecificOutput struct with new fields (ruleId, severity, remediation)
2. Add JSON Schema validation for output
3. Create AGENTS.md file for DCG
4. Add --format json to all subcommands
5. Standardize error codes and messages
6. Consider MCP server mode for direct integration

## Key Files

- src/hook.rs - Enhance HookSpecificOutput
- src/cli.rs - Add --format json to subcommands
- AGENTS.md - New: Machine-readable agent guide
- src/mcp.rs - New: Optional MCP server mode
- docs/json-schema.json - New: JSON Schema for outputs

## Success Criteria

- All commands support --format json
- JSON output includes ruleId, severity, remediation
- AGENTS.md provides agent-friendly documentation

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-e4fl.2","title":"[Agent] Create AGENTS.md machine-readable documentation","description":"# Task: Update/Enhance AGENTS.md Machine-Readable Documentation

## Background

AGENTS.md already exists in this project (735 lines). This task is to enhance it with DCG-specific machine-readable sections following emerging AGENTS.md conventions.

### Add Machine-Readable Sections
- JSON protocol documentation with examples
- Exit codes reference (structured)
- Allowlist bypass instructions
- Safe patterns quick reference
- Hook response examples

### Ensure Convention Compliance
- Follow emerging AGENTS.md standard (20k+ repos)
- Provide actionable info for AI agents
- Include code blocks agents can parse

## Note
DO NOT replace existing content. ADD new sections for DCG-specific documentation.

- Follows emerging AGENTS.md conventions
- Existing project guidelines preserved","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T14:19:54.297539836-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T15:38:23.984847991-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e4fl.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e4fl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T14:19:54.298868528-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
